case_name: Frances Koe, M.D., and Wills Valley Family Medicine, LLC v. Donna Ratliff, as personal representative of the Estate of Rhoda Gail McBride, (Appeal from DeKalb Circuit Court: CV-18-900266).

summary.brief: Frances Koe, M.D., and Wills Valley Family Medicine, LLC v. Donna Ratliff, as personal representative of the Estate of Rhoda Gail McBride, (Appeal from DeKalb Circuit Court: CV-18-900266).. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: On November 3, 2016, McBride sought treatment for leg pain at a medical clinic operated by Wills Valley.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The ultrasound revealed that McBride was suffering from a blood clot.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Koe instructed that McBride was to be prescribed Coumadin, a blood thinner.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ratliff testified that she was never informed that Coumadin is a blood thinner or about the necessity for regular blood tests.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Coumadin prescription bottle specifically instructs users of the medication to review the medication guide.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ratliff acknowledged that a medication guide could have been stapled to the prescription bag, but she claimed that she did not remember seeing it.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ratliff testified that she had not received any results of the blood test by December 12, 2016.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court ordered a new trial because, it concluded, the contributory-negligence defense and the jury's verdict therefore could not be supported by the evidence.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Court finds this to be especially true in a matter involving complex medical knowledge not known to a reasonably prudent layperson.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: We reverse the trial court's judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with leg pain and was referred for an ultrasound.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Leg pain with suspected vascular issue

requestables.name: Ultrasound
requestables.description: Ultrasound of the leg to check for blood clots

reveal.clinical_significance: Confirms presence of a blood clot requiring anticoagulation

requestables.name: PT/INR
requestables.description: Blood test to monitor blood thickness

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with leg pain and is referred for an ultrasound

key_events.event: Patient seen at clinic for leg pain

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: Ultrasound performed and blood clot diagnosed

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient diagnosed with a blood clot and prescribed Coumadin
decision_points.prompt: What follow-up care should be arranged for a patient starting on Coumadin?

options.description: Schedule regular PT/INR tests and educate patient on Coumadin risks
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care to prevent complications from anticoagulation therapy

options.description: Prescribe Coumadin without scheduling follow-up tests
options.clinical_reasoning: Neglects necessary monitoring for anticoagulation therapy

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant prescribed Coumadin without ensuring follow-up testing

expected_action_court.description: Regular PT/INR tests should have been scheduled
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony and standard anticoagulation management

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to ensure follow-up testing led to Coumadin toxicity
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Regular monitoring and patient education on Coumadin
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered a brain bleed and died

key_findings: Jury found sufficient evidence of contributory negligence
Trial court's order for a new trial was reversed

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Regular testing could have prevented Coumadin toxicity
